summary: Feel Like A Stranger opener shines, Comes A Time solos soar
blurb: The second set opens with a monster Feel Like A Stranger and flows into a powerful Ship Of Fools before an inspired Comes A Time solo. Jerry’s guitar is on fire while Phil lays down crisp bass and Brent’s organ electrifies the rare Johnny B. Goode finale. The night ends abruptly after a bottle shatters Billy’s snare.
review: Providence Civic Center, March 31 1986 – the Dead delivered a show that teeters between brilliance and chaos. A high‑energy first set tossed in classics like Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo and a loose (I'm A) Road Runner, but it never quite captured the spark of the second half. The band erupts with a monster Feel Like A Stranger that showcases Jerry’s “popping notes” and Phil’s thunderous bass, segueing into a tight Ship Of Fools. From there the night rolls into a jam‑filled space where Bill Kreutzmann’s drums are described as “UNREAL,” even as a rogue bottle crashes into his snare during Comes A Time, adding an unplanned burst of drama. Jerry’s solo on Comes A Time is called “beautiful and ripping,” while Brent’s B‑13 organ on Johnny B. Goode delivers a literal jolt of electricity, turning the unexpected cover into a memorable closing statement. The bottle incident forced an abrupt end – no encore, just a broken drum head and a stunned audience – but it also cemented this show as one of those wild, unforgettable moments that only the Dead could conjure.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across the twelve recordings, half label the show as positive (highlighting Feel Like A Stranger, Comes A Time, and strong drumming) while the other half are mixed, noting uneven pacing but still praising key moments. The consistent praise for specific songs and performances yields a modest positive adjustment (+0.8). No recurring negative comments were found to offset this.

best_recording.reason: Good audience tape that captures the full energy, clear drum sound despite the bottle smash, and highlights the standout Feel Like A Stranger and Comes A Time performances.

key_highlights: Monster Feel Like A Stranger opener with Jerry’s popping notes
Inspired solo on Comes A Time that many reviewers called beautiful
Dramatic bottle‑smash incident ending the show on a raw, unforgettable note
Electrifying Brent organ on the rare Johnny B. Goode close

band_performance.Jerry: Pops notes and delivers a fiery guitar throughout, with standout wah‑pedal work after the Stranger jam and a beautiful solo on Comes A Time.
band_performance.Phil: Acts as a “monster” bassist on the Stranger opener, laying down crisp, driving bass that fuels the second set jams.
band_performance.Brent: Provides an electrifying B‑13 organ burst on Johnny B. Goode, giving the cover a jolt of electricity.
band_performance.Billy: Drumming is described as UNREAL and great; even after a bottle shatters his snare he keeps a powerful groove that drives the set.
